zh.Galway, County Galway, Ireland: 愛爾蘭庫迪 高威高威
zh.lifeSummary: 當Richard an Fhorbhair de Burgh於1291年出生在愛爾蘭庫迪 高威時，他的父親William Laith de Burgh31歲，母親Finnoula O'Brien21歲。他和Ismania de Burgh至少有7個兒子。他去世於1360年在愛爾蘭享年69歲。

zh-hans.Galway, County Galway, Ireland: 爱尔兰庫迪 高威高威
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Richard an Fhorbhair de Burgh于1291年出生在爱尔兰庫迪 高威时，他的父亲William Laith de Burgh31岁，母亲Finnoula O'Brien21岁。他和Ismania de Burgh至少有7个儿子。他去世于1360年在爱尔兰享年69岁。

nameMeanings.name: Burgh
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Scottish: habitational name from one or other of the ten English places named Burgh in northern England and East Anglia, or the six named Brough in Derbyshire, Nottinghamshire, Westmorland, and Yorkshire. All these are from Old English burg, burh, Middle English burgh ‘massive earthwork, fortification; manor, borough’. Compare Bury . The placename Burgh is pronounced as borough. The surname Brough arose equally from places now called Burgh and Brough, and is the more common form of the surname in modern Britain, where it is pronounced bruff or broof (/brʊf/). The Scottish surname was probably brought in from Yorkshire. For the surname in Ireland see Burke .

variants.name: Burke
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Kieran, Siobhan, Aileen, Brennan, Brigid, Donovan, Liam, Murphy, Ulick, Aidan, Dermot.

English and Irish (of Anglo-Norman origin): habitational name from Burgh (Castle) in Suffolk, England. Burk represents an Anglo-Norman pronunciation of Old English burg, Middle English burgh ‘fortification’ (see Burgh ).
German: variant of   Burk  .

variants.name: Bury
meanings: English (Lancashire): habitational name from Bury in Lancashire (now part of Greater Manchester), or from some other similarly named place. The placename comes from the dative case, byrig, of Old English burh ‘fortified place’.

Walloon and French: habitational name from any of several places so named, in Wallonia (Belgium) and Oise (France). The placename is from Buriacum, the name of a Gallo-Roman estate, composed of the personal name Burius + the locative suffix -acum. This surname is also found in the Flemish part of Belgium.
South German: variant of   Buri  .

variants.name: Brough
meanings: English and Scottish: habitational name from any of various English places called Brough or Burgh. See   Burgh  .
Scottish: habitational name from Brough in Harray (Orkney).
Americanized form of German   Brauch   or of some other similar (like-sounding) surname.

variants.name: Burrow
meanings: English:

topographic name for someone who lived by a castle or manor house, or in a town, especially a chartered town or borough (Middle English burgh, Old English burg ‘fortified place, borough, manor’), or a habitational name for someone who came from a place so named because it was a fortified town or near an ancient fort, such as Burrow (Leicestershire, Lancashire), Burrough Green (Cambridgeshire), Brough (numerous counties), and Peterborough (Northamptonshire); see also Burgh .

topographic name for someone who lived on a hill or near a mound (Middle English bergh, Old English beorg), or a habitational name for someone who came from a place so named, such as Burrow (Devon, Somerset). Middle English bergh became throughly confused with burgh in 1 above. Compare Burrough .

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

nameMeanings.name: an
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: As a boy's name this represents a revival of Middle English Jan, a by‐form of John , or an adoption of the common European form with this spelling. As a girl's name it is a short form of names such as Janet and Janice .

variants.name: John
meanings: As a boy's name this represents a revival of Middle English Jan, a by‐form of John , or an adoption of the common European form with this spelling. As a girl's name it is a short form of names such as Janet and Janice .

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of the various boys' names with the first syllable Ed-, especially Edward .

variants.name: Edward
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + weard ‘guard’. This has been one of the most successful of all Old English names, in frequent use from before the Conquest to the present day, and even being exported into other European languages. It was the name of three Anglo-Saxon kings and has been borne by eight kings of England since the Norman Conquest. It is also the name of the youngest son of Queen Elizabeth II . The most influential early bearer was King Edward the Confessor ( ?1002–66 ; ruled 1042–66 ). In a troubled period of English history, he contrived to rule fairly and (for a time at any rate) firmly. But in the latter part of his reign he paid more attention to his religion than to his kingdom. He died childless, and his death sparked off conflicting claims to his throne, which were resolved by the victory of William the Conqueror at the Battle of Hastings. His memory was honoured by Normans and English alike, for his fairness and his piety. Edward's mother was Norman; he had spent part of his youth in Normandy; and William claimed to have been nominated by Edward as his successor. Edward was canonized in the 12th century, and came to be venerated throughout Europe as a model of a Christian king.

en.lifeSummary: When Richard an Fhorbhair de Burgh was born in 1291, in County Galway, Ireland, his father, William Laith de Burgh, was 31 and his mother, Finnoula O'Brien, was 21. He had at least 7 sons with Ismania de Burgh. He died in 1360, in Ireland, at the age of 69.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Richard an Fhorbhair de Burgh est né en 1291, à County Galway, Irlande, son père, William Laith de Burgh, avait 31 ans et sa mère, Finnoula O'Brien, avait 21 ans. Il a eu au moins 7 fils avec Ismania de Burgh. Il est décédé en 1360, à Irlande, à l’âge de 69 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Richard an Fhorbhair de Burgh 1291 in County Galway, Irland geboren wurde, war sein Vater William Laith de Burgh 31 und seine Mutter Finnoula O'Brien 21. Er hatte mit Ismania de Burgh mindestens 7 Söhne. Er starb 1360 im Alter von 69 Jahren in Irland.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Richard an Fhorbhair de Burgh è nato il nel 1291 nel County Galway, Irlanda, il suo padre, William Laith de Burgh, aveva 31 anni e il suo madre, Finnoula O'Brien, aveva 21 anni. Lui avuto almeno 7 figli con Ismania de Burgh. Morì nel 1360, nel Irlanda, all'età di 69 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Richard an Fhorbhair de Burghが1291年にアイルランドコウントイ ガルワイで生まれた時，父William Laith de Burghは31歳，母Finnoula O'Brienは21歳であった。彼はIsmania de Burghとの間に少なくとも7人の息子をもうけた。彼は1360年にアイルランドで69歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Richard an Fhorbhair de Burgh(이)가 1291년 아일랜드 카운티 갈웨이에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 William Laith de Burgh(은)는 31세였고, 그의 어머니 Finnoula O'Brien(은)는 21세였다. 그는 Ismania de Burgh(와)과 적어도 일곱 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1360년 아일랜드에서 69세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Richard an Fhorbhair de Burgh nasceu em 1291, em County Galway, Irlanda, seu pai, William Laith de Burgh, tinha 31 anos e sua mãe, Finnoula O'Brien, tinha 21 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 7 filhos com Ismania de Burgh. Ele faleceu em 1360, em Irlanda, com 69 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Richard an Fhorbhair de Burgh родился в 1291 г. в местности коунти галвеи, Ирландия, его отцу по имени William Laith de Burgh было 31 год, а матери, которую звали Finnoula O'Brien, 21. У него было по крайней мере 7 сыновей от Ismania de Burgh. Он умер в 1360 г. в местности Ирландия в возрасте 69 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Richard an Fhorbhair de Burgh nació en 1291, en County Galway, Irlanda, su papá, William Laith de Burgh, tenía 31 años y su mamá, Finnoula O'Brien, tenía 21 años. Tuvo por lo menos 7 hijos con Ismania de Burgh. Murió en 1360, en Irlanda, a la edad de 69 años.
